summary: Completed comprehensive Railway deployment readiness testing of Telegram Moderation Bot. All backend APIs, frontend functionality, and deployment configurations verified successfully.
passed_tests: Backend API GET /api/ returns root message
Backend API GET /api/health returns healthy status with bot_running=true and mongo_connected=true
Backend API GET /api/bot/status returns online status with uptime data
Backend API GET /api/bot/stats returns all stat fields (total_commands, mute_count, etc.)
Backend API GET /api/bot/commands returns all 8 command categories with correct counts
Frontend loads correctly with CASPERMODBOT header and terminal theme
Frontend displays ONLINE badge with green pulsing status indicator
Frontend shows 4 stat cards (Uptime, Commands, Groups, Version) with live backend data
All 8 accordion command sections render and expand properly (mute, unmute, kick, ban, admin, fun, owner, help)
Footer displays System Online status with owner @casperthe6ix info
Dockerfile exists and uses requirements.deploy.txt with $PORT environment variable
railway.toml exists with healthcheck config (/api/health endpoint)
.dockerignore properly excludes .env file from Docker image
server.py handles missing MONGO_URL gracefully with optional MongoDB setup
No hardcoded localhost/tokens found in server.py or bot.py (uses environment variables)
Deployment configuration properly configured for Railway with PORT env var support
success_percentage: Backend: 100% (5/5 tests passed), Frontend: 100% (8/8 tests passed), Deployment: 100% (6/6 checks passed)
